title: taraxacum duriense
intro: Taraxacum duriense, commonly known as the Spanish dandelion, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family. It has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its roots and leaves, which contain taraxasterol and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and diuretic effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing liver inflammation, improving digestion, and acting as a natural diuretic. Taraxacum duriense can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, as well as dried or used in capsules, to utilize its medicinal properties.
